text: Modern mathematics makes essential use of algebraic structures, which encapsulate patterns that can be instantiated in multiple settings. The subject provides various ways of defining such structures and constructing particular instances.
html: Modern mathematics makes essential use of algebraic structures, which encapsulate patterns that can be instantiated in multiple settings. The subject provides various ways of defining such structures and constructing particular instances.
zh: 现代数学本质上依赖代数结构；这些结构封装了可在多种情境中实例化的模式。数学提供了定义这类结构并构造其具体实例的多种方法。

text: Lean therefore provides corresponding ways of defining structures formally and working with them. You have already seen examples of algebraic structures in Lean, such as rings and lattices, which were discussed in Chapter 2 . This chapter will explain the mysterious square bracket annotations that you saw there, [Ring α] and [Lattice α] . It will also show you how to define and use algebraic structures on your own.
html: Lean therefore provides corresponding ways of defining structures formally and working with them. You have already seen examples of algebraic structures in Lean, such as rings and lattices, which were discussed in Chapter 2. This chapter will explain the mysterious square bracket annotations that you saw there, [Ring α] and [Lattice α]. It will also show you how to define and use algebraic structures on your own.
zh: 因此，Lean 也提供了形式化定义结构并使用结构的相应方式。你已经在 Lean 中见过一些代数结构的例子，例如Chapter 2中讨论过的环和格。本章将解释那里出现过的神秘方括号标注 [Ring α] 和 [Lattice α]，并说明如何自己定义和使用代数结构。

text: For more technical detail, you can consult Theorem Proving in Lean , and a paper by Anne Baanen, Use and abuse of instance parameters in the Lean mathematical library .
html: For more technical detail, you can consult Theorem Proving in Lean, and a paper by Anne Baanen, Use and abuse of instance parameters in the Lean mathematical library.
zh: 若需要更多技术细节，可以参考 Theorem Proving in Lean，以及 Anne Baanen 的论文 Use and abuse of instance parameters in the Lean mathematical library。

text: In the broadest sense of the term, a structure is a specification of a collection of data, possibly with constraints that the data is required to satisfy. An instance of the structure is a particular bundle of data satisfying the constraints. For example, we can specify that a point is a tuple of three real numbers:
html: In the broadest sense of the term, a structure is a specification of a collection of data, possibly with constraints that the data is required to satisfy. An instance of the structure is a particular bundle of data satisfying the constraints. For example, we can specify that a point is a tuple of three real numbers:
zh: 在最广泛的意义上，structure是对一组数据的规约，其中可能还包含这些数据必须满足的约束。该结构的一个instance，就是满足这些约束的一份具体数据包。例如，可以规定一个点是三个实数构成的元组：

text: The @[ext] annotation tells Lean to automatically generate theorems that can be used to prove that two instances of a structure are equal when their components are equal, a property known as extensionality .
html: The @[ext] annotation tells Lean to automatically generate theorems that can be used to prove that two instances of a structure are equal when their components are equal, a property known as extensionality.
zh: 标注 @[ext] 告诉 Lean 自动生成可用于证明结构两个实例相等的定理：只要它们的各个分量相等，就能证明实例相等。这一性质称为extensionality。

text: We can then define particular instances of the Point structure. Lean provides multiple ways of doing that.
html: We can then define particular instances of the Point structure. Lean provides multiple ways of doing that.
zh: 随后可以定义 Point 结构的具体实例。Lean 提供了多种做法。

text: In the first example, the fields of the structure are named explicitly. The function Point.mk referred to in the definition of myPoint4 is known as the constructor for the Point structure, because it serves to construct elements. You can specify a different name if you want, like build .
html: In the first example, the fields of the structure are named explicitly. The function Point.mk referred to in the definition of myPoint4 is known as the constructor for the Point structure, because it serves to construct elements. You can specify a different name if you want, like build.
zh: 在第一个例子中，结构的字段被显式命名。myPoint4 定义中提到的函数 Point.mk 称为 Point 结构的constructor，因为它用于构造元素。若愿意，也可以指定另一个名称，例如 build。

text: The next two examples show how to define functions on structures. Whereas the second example makes the Point.mk constructor explicit, the first example uses an anonymous constructor for brevity. Lean can infer the relevant constructor from the indicated type of add . It is conventional to put definitions and theorems associated with a structure like Point in a namespace with the same name. In the example below, because we have opened the Point namespace, the full name of add is Point.add . When the namespace is not open, we have to use the full name. But remember that it is often convenient to use anonymous projection notation, which allows us to write a.add b instead of Point.add a b . Lean interprets the former as the latter because a has type Point .
html: The next two examples show how to define functions on structures. Whereas the second example makes the Point.mk constructor explicit, the first example uses an anonymous constructor for brevity. Lean can infer the relevant constructor from the indicated type of add. It is conventional to put definitions and theorems associated with a structure like Point in a namespace with the same name. In the example below, because we have opened the Point namespace, the full name of add is Point.add. When the namespace is not open, we have to use the full name. But remember that it is often convenient to use anonymous projection notation, which allows us to write a.add b instead of Point.add a b. Lean interprets the former as the latter because a has type Point.
zh: 接下来的两个例子展示如何在结构上定义函数。第二个例子显式写出了构造子 Point.mk，而第一个例子为了简洁使用匿名构造子。Lean 可以根据所标明的 add 的类型推断出相关构造子。通常约定，将与 Point 这类结构相关的定义和定理放入同名命名空间。在下面的例子中，由于已经打开 Point 命名空间，add 的全名是 Point.add。当命名空间未打开时，必须使用全名。不过请记住，匿名投影记号常常很方便，它允许我们写 a.add b 而不是 Point.add a b。Lean 会把前者解释为后者，因为 a 的类型是 Point。

text: Below we will continue to put definitions in the relevant namespace, but we will leave the namespacing commands out of the quoted snippets. To prove properties of the addition function, we can use rw to expand the definition and ext to reduce an equation between two elements of the structure to equations between the components. Below we use the protected keyword so that the name of the theorem is Point.add_comm , even when the namespace is open. This is helpful when we want to avoid ambiguity with a generic theorem like add_comm .
html: Below we will continue to put definitions in the relevant namespace, but we will leave the namespacing commands out of the quoted snippets. To prove properties of the addition function, we can use rw to expand the definition and ext to reduce an equation between two elements of the structure to equations between the components. Below we use the protected keyword so that the name of the theorem is Point.add_comm, even when the namespace is open. This is helpful when we want to avoid ambiguity with a generic theorem like add_comm.
zh: 下面我们仍会把定义放在相关命名空间中，但在引用的代码片段里省略命名空间命令。为了证明加法函数的性质，可以用 rw 展开定义，并用 ext 把结构两个元素之间的等式化归为各分量之间的等式。下面我们使用关键字 protected，使得即使命名空间已经打开，该定理名称仍为 Point.add_comm。当我们希望避免与 add_comm 这样的通用定理产生歧义时，这很有用。

text: Because Lean can unfold definitions and simplify projections internally, sometimes the equations we want hold definitionally.
html: Because Lean can unfold definitions and simplify projections internally, sometimes the equations we want hold definitionally.
zh: 由于 Lean 可以在内部展开定义并化简投影，有时我们想要的等式会按定义成立。

text: It is also possible to define functions on structures using pattern matching, in a manner similar to the way we defined recursive functions in Section 5.2 . The definitions addAlt and addAlt' below are essentially the same; the only difference is that we use anonymous constructor notation in the second. Although it is sometimes convenient to define functions this way, and structural eta-reduction makes this alternative definitionally equivalent, it can make things less convenient in later proofs. In particular, rw [addAlt] leaves us with a messier goal view containing a match statement.
html: It is also possible to define functions on structures using pattern matching, in a manner similar to the way we defined recursive functions in Section 5.2. The definitions addAlt and addAlt' below are essentially the same; the only difference is that we use anonymous constructor notation in the second. Although it is sometimes convenient to define functions this way, and structural eta-reduction makes this alternative definitionally equivalent, it can make things less convenient in later proofs. In particular, rw [addAlt] leaves us with a messier goal view containing a match statement.
zh: 也可以用模式匹配在结构上定义函数，方式类似于我们在Section 5.2中定义递归函数的方式。下面的定义 addAlt 与 addAlt' 本质相同；唯一区别是第二个使用匿名构造子记号。虽然有时这样定义函数很方便，而且结构 η-约化使这个替代定义按定义等价，但在后续证明中它可能不那么方便。特别地，rw [addAlt] 会留下一个含有 match 语句、更混乱的目标视图。

text: Mathematical constructions often involve taking apart bundled information and putting it together again in different ways. It therefore makes sense that Lean and Mathlib offer so many ways of doing this efficiently. As an exercise, try proving that Point.add is associative. Then define scalar multiplication for a point and show that it distributes over addition.
html: Mathematical constructions often involve taking apart bundled information and putting it together again in different ways. It therefore makes sense that Lean and Mathlib offer so many ways of doing this efficiently. As an exercise, try proving that Point.add is associative. Then define scalar multiplication for a point and show that it distributes over addition.
zh: 数学构造常常需要拆解已打包的信息，再以不同方式重新组合。因此 Lean 和 Mathlib 提供许多高效完成这件事的方法，是很自然的。作为练习，请尝试证明 Point.add 满足结合律。然后为点定义标量乘法，并证明它对加法满足分配律。

text: Using structures is only the first step on the road to algebraic abstraction. We don’t yet have a way to link Point.add to the generic + symbol, or to connect Point.add_comm and Point.add_assoc to the generic add_comm and add_assoc theorems. These tasks belong to the algebraic aspect of using structures, and we will explain how to carry them out in the next section. For now, just think of a structure as a way of bundling together objects and information.
html: Using structures is only the first step on the road to algebraic abstraction. We don’t yet have a way to link Point.add to the generic + symbol, or to connect Point.add_comm and Point.add_assoc to the generic add_comm and add_assoc theorems. These tasks belong to the algebraic aspect of using structures, and we will explain how to carry them out in the next section. For now, just think of a structure as a way of bundling together objects and information.
zh: 使用结构只是通向代数抽象的第一步。我们还没有办法把 Point.add 与通用符号 + 关联起来，也没有办法把 Point.add_comm 和 Point.add_assoc 与通用定理 add_comm 和 add_assoc 关联起来。这些任务属于使用结构的algebraic方面，我们将在下一节解释如何完成。现在，只需把结构看作打包对象和信息的一种方式。

text: It is especially useful that a structure can specify not only data types but also constraints that the data must satisfy. In Lean, the latter are represented as fields of type Prop . For example, the standard 2-simplex is defined to be the set of points \((x, y, z)\) satisfying \(x ≥ 0\) , \(y ≥ 0\) , \(z ≥ 0\) , and \(x + y + z = 1\) . If you are not familiar with the notion, you should draw a picture, and convince yourself that this set is the equilateral triangle in three-space with vertices \((1, 0, 0)\) , \((0, 1, 0)\) , and \((0, 0, 1)\) , together with its interior. We can represent it in Lean as follows:
html: It is especially useful that a structure can specify not only data types but also constraints that the data must satisfy. In Lean, the latter are represented as fields of type Prop. For example, the standard 2-simplex is defined to be the set of points \((x, y, z)\) satisfying \(x ≥ 0\), \(y ≥ 0\), \(z ≥ 0\), and \(x + y + z = 1\). If you are not familiar with the notion, you should draw a picture, and convince yourself that this set is the equilateral triangle in three-space with vertices \((1, 0, 0)\), \((0, 1, 0)\), and \((0, 0, 1)\), together with its interior. We can represent it in Lean as follows:
zh: 结构特别有用的一点是：它不仅能指定数据类型，也能指定这些数据必须满足的约束。在 Lean 中，后者表示为类型为 Prop 的字段。例如，standard 2-simplex定义为满足 \(x ≥ 0\)、\(y ≥ 0\)、\(z ≥ 0\) 和 \(x + y + z = 1\) 的点 \((x, y, z)\) 的集合。如果你不熟悉这个概念，应当画一幅图，并说服自己这个集合就是三维空间中以 \((1, 0, 0)\)、\((0, 1, 0)\) 和 \((0, 0, 1)\) 为顶点的等边三角形连同其内部。在 Lean 中可以如下表示它：

text: Notice that the last four fields refer to x , y , and z , that is, the first three fields. We can define a map from the two-simplex to itself that swaps x and y :
html: Notice that the last four fields refer to x, y, and z, that is, the first three fields. We can define a map from the two-simplex to itself that swaps x and y:
zh: 注意，最后四个字段引用了 x、y 和 z，也就是前三个字段。可以定义一个从二单纯形到自身的映射，交换 x 与 y：

text: More interestingly, we can compute the midpoint of two points on the simplex. We have added the phrase noncomputable section at the beginning of this file in order to use division on the real numbers.
html: More interestingly, we can compute the midpoint of two points on the simplex. We have added the phrase noncomputable section at the beginning of this file in order to use division on the real numbers.
zh: 更有趣的是，可以计算单纯形上两个点的中点。为了使用实数上的除法，我们在本文件开头加入了短语 noncomputable section。

text: Here we have established x_nonneg , y_nonneg , and z_nonneg with concise proof terms, but establish sum_eq in tactic mode, using by .
html: Here we have established x_nonneg, y_nonneg, and z_nonneg with concise proof terms, but establish sum_eq in tactic mode, using by.
zh: 这里我们用简短的证明项建立了 x_nonneg、y_nonneg 和 z_nonneg，但用策略模式通过 by 建立 sum_eq。

text: Given a parameter \(\lambda\) satisfying \(0 \le \lambda \le 1\) , we can take the weighted average \(\lambda a + (1 - \lambda) b\) of two points \(a\) and \(b\) in the standard 2-simplex. We challenge you to define that function, in analogy to the midpoint function above.
html: Given a parameter \(\lambda\) satisfying \(0 \le \lambda \le 1\), we can take the weighted average \(\lambda a + (1 - \lambda) b\) of two points \(a\) and \(b\) in the standard 2-simplex. We challenge you to define that function, in analogy to the midpoint function above.
zh: 给定满足 \(0 \le \lambda \le 1\) 的参数 \(\lambda\)，可以取标准 2-单纯形中两点 \(a\) 和 \(b\) 的加权平均 \(\lambda a + (1 - \lambda) b\)。请仿照上面的 midpoint 函数定义这个函数。

text: Structures can depend on parameters. For example, we can generalize the standard 2-simplex to the standard \(n\) -simplex for any \(n\) . At this stage, you don’t have to know anything about the type Fin n except that it has \(n\) elements, and that Lean knows how to sum over it.
html: Structures can depend on parameters. For example, we can generalize the standard 2-simplex to the standard \(n\)-simplex for any \(n\). At this stage, you don’t have to know anything about the type Fin n except that it has \(n\) elements, and that Lean knows how to sum over it.
zh: 结构可以依赖参数。例如，可以把标准 2-单纯形推广为任意 \(n\) 的标准 \(n\)-单纯形。在这个阶段，你不需要知道类型 Fin n 的任何内容，只需知道它有 \(n\) 个元素，并且 Lean 知道如何在其上求和。

text: As an exercise, see if you can define the weighted average of two points in the standard \(n\) -simplex. You can use Finset.sum_add_distrib and Finset.mul_sum to manipulate the relevant sums.
html: As an exercise, see if you can define the weighted average of two points in the standard \(n\)-simplex. You can use Finset.sum_add_distrib and Finset.mul_sum to manipulate the relevant sums.
zh: 作为练习，请看看能否定义标准 \(n\)-单纯形中两点的加权平均。可以使用 Finset.sum_add_distrib 和 Finset.mul_sum 操作相关求和。

text: We have seen that structures can be used to bundle together data and properties. Interestingly, they can also be used to bundle together properties without the data. For example, the next structure, IsLinear , bundles together the two components of linearity.
html: We have seen that structures can be used to bundle together data and properties. Interestingly, they can also be used to bundle together properties without the data. For example, the next structure, IsLinear, bundles together the two components of linearity.
zh: 我们已经看到，结构可以把数据和性质打包在一起。有趣的是，它们也可以只打包性质而不包含数据。例如，下一个结构 IsLinear 把线性的两个组成部分打包在一起。

text: It is worth pointing out that structures are not the only way to bundle together data. The Point data structure can be defined using the generic type product, and IsLinear can be defined with a simple and .
html: It is worth pointing out that structures are not the only way to bundle together data. The Point data structure can be defined using the generic type product, and IsLinear can be defined with a simple and.
zh: 值得指出的是，结构并不是打包数据的唯一方式。数据结构 Point 可以用通用的类型乘积定义，而 IsLinear 可以用简单的 and 定义。

text: Generic type constructions can even be used in place of structures with dependencies between their components. For example, the subtype construction combines a piece of data with a property. You can think of the type PReal in the next example as being the type of positive real numbers. Any x : PReal has two components: the value, and the property of being positive. You can access these components as x.val , which has type ℝ , and x.property , which represents the fact 0 < x.val .
html: Generic type constructions can even be used in place of structures with dependencies between their components. For example, the subtype construction combines a piece of data with a property. You can think of the type PReal in the next example as being the type of positive real numbers. Any x : PReal has two components: the value, and the property of being positive. You can access these components as x.val, which has type ℝ, and x.property, which represents the fact 0 < x.val.
zh: 通用类型构造甚至可以替代分量之间存在依赖的结构。例如，subtype构造把一份数据与一个性质结合起来。可以把下一个例子中的类型 PReal 看作正实数类型。任意 x : PReal 都有两个分量：它的值，以及它为正的性质。可以通过 x.val 访问前者，其类型为 ℝ；通过 x.property 访问后者，它表示事实 0 < x.val。

text: We could have used subtypes to define the standard 2-simplex, as well as the standard \(n\) -simplex for an arbitrary \(n\) .
html: We could have used subtypes to define the standard 2-simplex, as well as the standard \(n\)-simplex for an arbitrary \(n\).
zh: 我们本来也可以用子类型定义标准 2-单纯形，以及任意 \(n\) 的标准 \(n\)-单纯形。

text: Similarly, Sigma types are generalizations of ordered pairs, whereby the type of the second component depends on the type of the first.
html: Similarly, Sigma types are generalizations of ordered pairs, whereby the type of the second component depends on the type of the first.
zh: 类似地，Sigma types是有序对的推广，其中第二个分量的类型依赖于第一个分量的类型。

text: Given s : StdSimplex , the first component s.fst is a natural number, and the second component is an element of the corresponding simplex StandardSimplex s.fst . The difference between a Sigma type and a subtype is that the second component of a Sigma type is data rather than a proposition.
html: Given s : StdSimplex, the first component s.fst is a natural number, and the second component is an element of the corresponding simplex StandardSimplex s.fst. The difference between a Sigma type and a subtype is that the second component of a Sigma type is data rather than a proposition.
zh: 给定 s : StdSimplex，第一个分量 s.fst 是一个自然数，第二个分量是相应单纯形 StandardSimplex s.fst 的一个元素。Sigma 类型与子类型的区别在于，Sigma 类型的第二个分量是数据，而不是命题。

text: But even though we can use products, subtypes, and Sigma types instead of structures, using structures has a number of advantages. Defining a structure abstracts away the underlying representation and provides custom names for the functions that access the components. This makes proofs more robust: proofs that rely only on the interface to a structure will generally continue to work when we change the definition, as long as we redefine the old accessors in terms of the new definition. Moreover, as we are about to see, Lean provides support for weaving structures together into a rich, interconnected hierarchy, and for managing the interactions between them.
html: But even though we can use products, subtypes, and Sigma types instead of structures, using structures has a number of advantages. Defining a structure abstracts away the underlying representation and provides custom names for the functions that access the components. This makes proofs more robust: proofs that rely only on the interface to a structure will generally continue to work when we change the definition, as long as we redefine the old accessors in terms of the new definition. Moreover, as we are about to see, Lean provides support for weaving structures together into a rich, interconnected hierarchy, and for managing the interactions between them.
zh: 不过，尽管可以用乘积、子类型和 Sigma 类型替代结构，使用结构仍有许多优势。定义结构会抽象掉底层表示，并为访问各分量的函数提供自定义名称。这使证明更加稳健：只依赖结构接口的证明，通常在我们改变定义后仍能继续工作，只要把旧访问器用新定义重新定义出来。此外，正如我们马上将看到的，Lean 支持把结构编织成丰富且相互关联的层级体系，并管理它们之间的相互作用。

text: To clarify what we mean by the phrase algebraic structure , it will help to consider some examples.
html: To clarify what we mean by the phrase algebraic structure, it will help to consider some examples.
zh: 为了澄清“algebraic structure”这一短语的含义，考虑一些例子会很有帮助。

text: In each of these examples, the elements of the structure belong to a set, the carrier set , that sometimes stands proxy for the entire structure. For example, when we say “let \(G\) be a group” and then “let \(g \in G\) ,” we are using \(G\) to stand for both the structure and its carrier. Not every algebraic structure is associated with a single carrier set in this way. For example, a bipartite graph involves a relation between two sets, as does a Galois connection . A category also involves two sets of interest, commonly called the objects and the morphisms .
html: In each of these examples, the elements of the structure belong to a set, the carrier set, that sometimes stands proxy for the entire structure. For example, when we say “let \(G\) be a group” and then “let \(g \in G\),” we are using \(G\) to stand for both the structure and its carrier. Not every algebraic structure is associated with a single carrier set in this way. For example, a bipartite graph involves a relation between two sets, as does a Galois connection. A category also involves two sets of interest, commonly called the objects and the morphisms.
zh: 在这些例子中，结构的元素属于一个集合，即carrier set；它有时代表整个结构。例如，当我们说“令 \(G\) 为一个群”，随后又说“令 \(g \in G\)”时，我们同时用 \(G\) 表示该结构及其载集。并非每个代数结构都以这种方式关联到单个载集。例如，bipartite graph涉及两个集合之间的关系，Galois connection也是如此。category也涉及两个重要集合，通常称为objects和morphisms。

text: The examples indicate some of the things that a proof assistant has to do in order to support algebraic reasoning. First, it needs to recognize concrete instances of structures. The number systems \(\mathbb{Z}\) , \(\mathbb{Q}\) , and \(\mathbb{R}\) are all ordered rings, and we should be able to apply a generic theorem about ordered rings in any of these instances. Sometimes a concrete set may be an instance of a structure in more than one way. For example, in addition to the usual topology on \(\mathbb{R}\) , which forms the basis for real analysis, we can also consider the discrete topology on \(\mathbb{R}\) , in which every set is open.
html: The examples indicate some of the things that a proof assistant has to do in order to support algebraic reasoning. First, it needs to recognize concrete instances of structures. The number systems \(\mathbb{Z}\), \(\mathbb{Q}\), and \(\mathbb{R}\) are all ordered rings, and we should be able to apply a generic theorem about ordered rings in any of these instances. Sometimes a concrete set may be an instance of a structure in more than one way. For example, in addition to the usual topology on \(\mathbb{R}\), which forms the basis for real analysis, we can also consider the discrete topology on \(\mathbb{R}\), in which every set is open.
zh: 这些例子显示，为了支持代数推理，证明助手必须完成一些任务。首先，它需要识别结构的具体实例。数系 \(\mathbb{Z}\)、\(\mathbb{Q}\) 和 \(\mathbb{R}\) 都是有序环，因此我们应能在这些任一实例中应用关于有序环的通用定理。有时，一个具体集合可以以不止一种方式成为某结构的实例。例如，除了作为实分析基础的 \(\mathbb{R}\) 上通常拓扑之外，我们还可以考虑 \(\mathbb{R}\) 上的discrete拓扑，其中每个集合都是开集。

text: Second, a proof assistant needs to support generic notation on structures. In Lean, the notation * is used for multiplication in all the usual number systems, as well as for multiplication in generic groups and rings. When we use an expression like f x * y , Lean has to use information about the types of f , x , and y to determine which multiplication we have in mind.
html: Second, a proof assistant needs to support generic notation on structures. In Lean, the notation * is used for multiplication in all the usual number systems, as well as for multiplication in generic groups and rings. When we use an expression like f x * y, Lean has to use information about the types of f, x, and y to determine which multiplication we have in mind.
zh: 其次，证明助手需要支持结构上的通用记号。在 Lean 中，记号 * 用于所有通常数系中的乘法，也用于通用群和环中的乘法。当我们使用类似 f x * y 的表达式时，Lean 必须利用关于 f、x 和 y 的类型信息来判定我们意指哪一种乘法。

text: Third, it needs to deal with the fact that structures can inherit definitions, theorems, and notation from other structures in various ways. Some structures extend others by adding more axioms. A commutative ring is still a ring, so any definition that makes sense in a ring also makes sense in a commutative ring, and any theorem that holds in a ring also holds in a commutative ring. Some structures extend others by adding more data. For example, the additive part of any ring is an additive group. The ring structure adds a multiplication and an identity, as well as axioms that govern them and relate them to the additive part. Sometimes we can define one structure in terms of another. Any metric space has a canonical topology associated with it, the metric space topology , and there are various topologies that can be associated with any linear ordering.
html: Third, it needs to deal with the fact that structures can inherit definitions, theorems, and notation from other structures in various ways. Some structures extend others by adding more axioms. A commutative ring is still a ring, so any definition that makes sense in a ring also makes sense in a commutative ring, and any theorem that holds in a ring also holds in a commutative ring. Some structures extend others by adding more data. For example, the additive part of any ring is an additive group. The ring structure adds a multiplication and an identity, as well as axioms that govern them and relate them to the additive part. Sometimes we can define one structure in terms of another. Any metric space has a canonical topology associated with it, the metric space topology, and there are various topologies that can be associated with any linear ordering.
zh: 第三，它需要处理这样一个事实：结构能够以多种方式从其他结构继承定义、定理和记号。有些结构通过增加更多公理来扩展其他结构。交换环仍然是环，所以任何在环中有意义的定义在交换环中也有意义，任何在环中成立的定理在交换环中也成立。有些结构通过增加更多数据来扩展其他结构。例如，任意环的加法部分是一个加法群。环结构增加了乘法和单位元，以及支配它们并把它们同加法部分联系起来的公理。有时我们可以用一个结构定义另一个结构。任意度量空间都有一个与之关联的典范拓扑，即metric space topology；任意线性序也可以关联多种拓扑。

text: Finally, it is important to keep in mind that mathematics allows us to use functions and operations to define structures in the same way we use functions and operations to define numbers. Products and powers of groups are again groups. For every \(n\) , the integers modulo \(n\) form a ring, and for every \(k > 0\) , the \(k \times k\) matrices of polynomials with coefficients in that ring again form a ring. Thus we can calculate with structures just as easily as we can calculate with their elements. This means that algebraic structures lead dual lives in mathematics, as containers for collections of objects and as objects in their own right. A proof assistant has to accommodate this dual role.
html: Finally, it is important to keep in mind that mathematics allows us to use functions and operations to define structures in the same way we use functions and operations to define numbers. Products and powers of groups are again groups. For every \(n\), the integers modulo \(n\) form a ring, and for every \(k > 0\), the \(k \times k\) matrices of polynomials with coefficients in that ring again form a ring. Thus we can calculate with structures just as easily as we can calculate with their elements. This means that algebraic structures lead dual lives in mathematics, as containers for collections of objects and as objects in their own right. A proof assistant has to accommodate this dual role.
zh: 最后，需要牢记，数学允许我们用函数和运算定义结构，正如我们用函数和运算定义数一样。群的乘积和幂仍是群。对每个 \(n\)，模 \(n\) 整数形成一个环；对每个 \(k > 0\)，以该环中元素为系数的多项式的 \(k \times k\) 矩阵又形成一个环。因此，我们可以像计算结构的元素一样方便地计算结构本身。这意味着代数结构在数学中具有双重身份：既是对象集合的容器，又是独立的对象。证明助手必须适应这种双重角色。

text: When dealing with elements of a type that has an algebraic structure associated with it, a proof assistant needs to recognize the structure and find the relevant definitions, theorems, and notation. All this should sound like a lot of work, and it is. But Lean uses a small collection of fundamental mechanisms to carry out these tasks. The goal of this section is to explain these mechanisms and show you how to use them.
html: When dealing with elements of a type that has an algebraic structure associated with it, a proof assistant needs to recognize the structure and find the relevant definitions, theorems, and notation. All this should sound like a lot of work, and it is. But Lean uses a small collection of fundamental mechanisms to carry out these tasks. The goal of this section is to explain these mechanisms and show you how to use them.
zh: 在处理一个具有相关代数结构的类型的元素时，证明助手需要识别该结构，并找到相关定义、定理和记号。这些听起来像是大量工作，而事实也确实如此。不过 Lean 使用一小组基本机制来完成这些任务。本节的目标就是解释这些机制，并展示如何使用它们。

text: The first ingredient is almost too obvious to mention: formally speaking, algebraic structures are structures in the sense of Section 7.1 . An algebraic structure is a specification of a bundle of data satisfying some axiomatic hypotheses, and we saw in Section 7.1 that this is exactly what the structure command is designed to accommodate. It’s a marriage made in heaven!
html: The first ingredient is almost too obvious to mention: formally speaking, algebraic structures are structures in the sense of Section 7.1. An algebraic structure is a specification of a bundle of data satisfying some axiomatic hypotheses, and we saw in Section 7.1 that this is exactly what the structure command is designed to accommodate. It’s a marriage made in heaven!
zh: 第一个成分几乎显而易见：从形式上说，代数结构就是Section 7.1意义下的结构。代数结构是一组满足某些公理假设的数据的规约，而我们在Section 7.1中已经看到，structure 命令正是为容纳这种内容而设计的。这种配合可谓恰到好处。

text: Given a data type α , we can define a group structure on α as follows.
html: Given a data type α, we can define a group structure on α as follows.
zh: 给定一个数据类型 α，可以如下在 α 上定义群结构。

text: Notice that the type α is a parameter in the definition of Group₁ . So you should think of an object struc : Group₁ α as being a group structure on α . We saw in Section 2.2 that the counterpart mul_inv_cancel to inv_mul_cancel follows from the other group axioms, so there is no need to add it to the definition.
html: Notice that the type α is a parameter in the definition of Group₁. So you should think of an object struc : Group₁ α as being a group structure on α. We saw in Section 2.2 that the counterpart mul_inv_cancel to inv_mul_cancel follows from the other group axioms, so there is no need to add it to the definition.
zh: 注意，类型 α 是定义 Group₁ 中的一个parameter。因此，应把对象 struc : Group₁ α 理解为 α 上的一个群结构。我们在Section 2.2中看到，inv_mul_cancel 的对应形式 mul_inv_cancel 可由其他群公理推出，因此无需把它加入定义。

text: This definition of a group is similar to the definition of Group in Mathlib, and we have chosen the name Group₁ to distinguish our version. If you write #check Group and ctrl-click on the definition, you will see that the Mathlib version of Group is defined to extend another structure; we will explain how to do that later. If you type #print Group you will also see that the Mathlib version of Group has a number of extra fields. For reasons we will explain later, sometimes it is useful to add redundant information to a structure, so that there are additional fields for objects and functions that can be defined from the core data. Don’t worry about that for now. Rest assured that our simplified version Group₁ is morally the same as the definition of a group that Mathlib uses.
html: This definition of a group is similar to the definition of Group in Mathlib, and we have chosen the name Group₁ to distinguish our version. If you write #check Group and ctrl-click on the definition, you will see that the Mathlib version of Group is defined to extend another structure; we will explain how to do that later. If you type #print Group you will also see that the Mathlib version of Group has a number of extra fields. For reasons we will explain later, sometimes it is useful to add redundant information to a structure, so that there are additional fields for objects and functions that can be defined from the core data. Don’t worry about that for now. Rest assured that our simplified version Group₁ is morally the same as the definition of a group that Mathlib uses.
zh: 这个群定义类似于 Mathlib 中 Group 的定义；我们选择名称 Group₁ 来区分自己的版本。如果写 #check Group 并按住 Ctrl 点击定义，会看到 Mathlib 版本的 Group 被定义为扩展另一个结构；稍后会解释如何做到这一点。如果输入 #print Group，还会看到 Mathlib 版本的 Group 有若干额外字段。出于稍后要解释的原因，有时向结构中加入冗余信息是有用的，也就是为可由核心数据定义出的对象和函数增加额外字段。现在不必担心这一点。可以放心，我们的简化版本 Group₁ 在数学本质上与 Mathlib 使用的群定义相同。

text: It is sometimes useful to bundle the type together with the structure, and Mathlib also contains a definition of a Grp structure that is equivalent to the following:
html: It is sometimes useful to bundle the type together with the structure, and Mathlib also contains a definition of a Grp structure that is equivalent to the following:
zh: 有时把类型与结构打包在一起是有用的，Mathlib 也包含一个与下述定义等价的 Grp 结构定义：

text: The Mathlib version is found in Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic , and you can #check it if you add this to the imports at the beginning of the examples file.
html: The Mathlib version is found in Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic, and you can #check it if you add this to the imports at the beginning of the examples file.
zh: Mathlib 版本位于 Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic 中；若把它加入示例文件开头的导入中，就可以对它执行 #check。

text: For reasons that will become clearer below, it is more often useful to keep the type α separate from the structure Group α . We refer to the two objects together as a partially bundled structure , since the representation combines most, but not all, of the components into one structure. It is common in Mathlib to use capital roman letters like G for a type when it is used as the carrier type for a group.
html: For reasons that will become clearer below, it is more often useful to keep the type α separate from the structure Group α. We refer to the two objects together as a partially bundled structure, since the representation combines most, but not all, of the components into one structure. It is common in Mathlib to use capital roman letters like G for a type when it is used as the carrier type for a group.
zh: 出于下面会逐渐清楚的原因，更常用的做法是把类型 α 与结构 Group α 分开。我们把这两个对象合称为partially bundled structure，因为这种表示把大多数分量、但不是全部分量合并到一个结构中。在 Mathlib 中，当某个类型用作群的载体类型时，通常用像 G 这样的大写罗马字母表示它。

text: Let’s construct a group by defining an element of the Group₁ type. For any pair of types α and β , Mathlib defines the type Equiv α β of equivalences between α and β . Mathlib also defines the suggestive notation α ≃ β for this type. An element f : α ≃ β is a bijection between α and β represented by four components: a function f.toFun from α to β , the inverse function f.invFun from β to α , and two properties that specify these functions are indeed inverse to one another.
html: Let’s construct a group by defining an element of the Group₁ type. For any pair of types α and β, Mathlib defines the type Equiv α β of equivalences between α and β. Mathlib also defines the suggestive notation α ≃ β for this type. An element f : α ≃ β is a bijection between α and β represented by four components: a function f.toFun from α to β, the inverse function f.invFun from β to α, and two properties that specify these functions are indeed inverse to one another.
zh: 让我们通过定义 Group₁ 类型的一个元素来构造一个群。对于任意一对类型 α 和 β，Mathlib 定义了类型 Equiv α β，表示 α 与 β 之间的equivalences。Mathlib 还为这个类型定义了直观的记号 α ≃ β。元素 f : α ≃ β 是 α 与 β 之间的一个双射，由四个分量表示：从 α 到 β 的函数 f.toFun，从 β 到 α 的逆函数 f.invFun，以及说明这两个函数确实互为逆的两个性质。

text: Notice the creative naming of the last three constructions. We think of the identity function Equiv.refl , the inverse operation Equiv.symm , and the composition operation Equiv.trans as explicit evidence that the property of being in bijective correspondence is an equivalence relation.
html: Notice the creative naming of the last three constructions. We think of the identity function Equiv.refl, the inverse operation Equiv.symm, and the composition operation Equiv.trans as explicit evidence that the property of being in bijective correspondence is an equivalence relation.
zh: 注意最后三个构造名称颇具创造性。我们把恒等函数 Equiv.refl、取逆操作 Equiv.symm 和复合操作 Equiv.trans 看作“双射对应”这一性质为等价关系的显式证据。

text: Notice also that f.trans g requires composing the forward functions in reverse order. Mathlib has declared a coercion from Equiv α β to the function type α → β , so we can omit writing .toFun and have Lean insert it for us.
html: Notice also that f.trans g requires composing the forward functions in reverse order. Mathlib has declared a coercion from Equiv α β to the function type α → β, so we can omit writing .toFun and have Lean insert it for us.
zh: 还要注意，f.trans g 要求按相反顺序复合正向函数。Mathlib 已经声明了从 Equiv α β 到函数类型 α → β 的coercion，因此可以省略 .toFun，让 Lean 为我们插入它。

text: Mathlib also defines the type Perm α of equivalences between α and itself.
html: Mathlib also defines the type Perm α of equivalences between α and itself.
zh: Mathlib 还定义了类型 Perm α，表示 α 到自身的等价。

text: It should be clear that Equiv.Perm α forms a group under composition of equivalences. We orient things so that mul f g is equal to g.trans f , whose forward function is f ∘ g . In other words, multiplication is what we ordinarily think of as composition of the bijections. Here we define this group:
html: It should be clear that Equiv.Perm α forms a group under composition of equivalences. We orient things so that mul f g is equal to g.trans f, whose forward function is f ∘ g. In other words, multiplication is what we ordinarily think of as composition of the bijections. Here we define this group:
zh: 显然，Equiv.Perm α 在等价复合下形成一个群。我们约定方向，使 mul f g 等于 g.trans f，后者的正向函数是 f ∘ g。换言之，乘法就是通常意义下双射的复合。下面定义这个群：

text: In fact, Mathlib defines exactly this Group structure on Equiv.Perm α in the file Algebra.Group.End . As always, you can hover over the theorems used in the definition of permGroup to see their statements, and you can jump to their definitions in the original file to learn more about how they are implemented.
html: In fact, Mathlib defines exactly this Group structure on Equiv.Perm α in the file Algebra.Group.End. As always, you can hover over the theorems used in the definition of permGroup to see their statements, and you can jump to their definitions in the original file to learn more about how they are implemented.
zh: 事实上，Mathlib 在文件 Algebra.Group.End 中为 Equiv.Perm α 定义的正是这个 Group 结构。照常，可以把鼠标悬停在 permGroup 定义中使用的定理上查看其陈述，也可以跳转到原文件中的定义，进一步了解它们如何实现。

text: In ordinary mathematics, we generally think of notation as independent of structure. For example, we can consider groups \((G_1, \cdot, 1, \cdot^{-1})\) , \((G_2, \circ, e, i(\cdot))\) , and \((G_3, +, 0, -)\) . In the first case, we write the binary operation as \(\cdot\) , the identity as \(1\) , and the inverse function as \(x \mapsto x^{-1}\) . In the second and third cases, we use the notational alternatives shown. When we formalize the notion of a group in Lean, however, the notation is more tightly linked to the structure. In Lean, the components of any Group are named mul , one , and inv , and in a moment we will see how multiplicative notation is set up to refer to them. If we want to use additive notation, we instead use an isomorphic structure AddGroup (the structure underlying additive groups). Its components are named add , zero , and neg , and the associated notation is what you would expect it to be.
html: In ordinary mathematics, we generally think of notation as independent of structure. For example, we can consider groups \((G_1, \cdot, 1, \cdot^{-1})\), \((G_2, \circ, e, i(\cdot))\), and \((G_3, +, 0, -)\). In the first case, we write the binary operation as \(\cdot\), the identity as \(1\), and the inverse function as \(x \mapsto x^{-1}\). In the second and third cases, we use the notational alternatives shown. When we formalize the notion of a group in Lean, however, the notation is more tightly linked to the structure. In Lean, the components of any Group are named mul, one, and inv, and in a moment we will see how multiplicative notation is set up to refer to them. If we want to use additive notation, we instead use an isomorphic structure AddGroup (the structure underlying additive groups). Its components are named add, zero, and neg, and the associated notation is what you would expect it to be.
zh: 在通常数学中，我们一般认为记号独立于结构。例如，可以考虑群 \((G_1, \cdot, 1, \cdot^{-1})\)、\((G_2, \circ, e, i(\cdot))\) 和 \((G_3, +, 0, -)\)。在第一种情况下，把二元运算写作 \(\cdot\)，把单位元写作 \(1\)，把取逆函数写作 \(x \mapsto x^{-1}\)。在第二和第三种情况下，我们使用所示的替代记号。然而，当在 Lean 中形式化群的概念时，记号与结构联系得更紧密。在 Lean 中，任意 Group 的分量命名为 mul、one 和 inv，稍后我们会看到乘法记号如何设置为引用这些分量。如果想使用加法记号，则改用同构的结构 AddGroup（即加法群底层的结构）。它的分量命名为 add、zero 和 neg，相应记号也正如预期。

text: Recall the type Point that we defined in Section 7.1 , and the addition function that we defined there. These definitions are reproduced in the examples file that accompanies this section. As an exercise, define an AddGroup₁ structure that is similar to the Group₁ structure we defined above, except that it uses the additive naming scheme just described. Define negation and a zero on the Point data type, and define the AddGroup₁ structure on Point .
html: Recall the type Point that we defined in Section 7.1, and the addition function that we defined there. These definitions are reproduced in the examples file that accompanies this section. As an exercise, define an AddGroup₁ structure that is similar to the Group₁ structure we defined above, except that it uses the additive naming scheme just described. Define negation and a zero on the Point data type, and define the AddGroup₁ structure on Point.
zh: 回忆在Section 7.1中定义的类型 Point，以及在那里定义的加法函数。这些定义在本节配套的示例文件中再次给出。作为练习，请定义一个 AddGroup₁ 结构，它类似于上面定义的 Group₁ 结构，只是使用刚才描述的加法命名方案。请在数据类型 Point 上定义取负和零元，并在 Point 上定义 AddGroup₁ 结构。

text: We are making progress. Now we know how to define algebraic structures in Lean, and we know how to define instances of those structures. But we also want to associate notation with structures so that we can use it with each instance. Moreover, we want to arrange it so that we can define an operation on a structure and use it with any particular instance, and we want to arrange it so that we can prove a theorem about a structure and use it with any instance.
html: We are making progress. Now we know how to define algebraic structures in Lean, and we know how to define instances of those structures. But we also want to associate notation with structures so that we can use it with each instance. Moreover, we want to arrange it so that we can define an operation on a structure and use it with any particular instance, and we want to arrange it so that we can prove a theorem about a structure and use it with any instance.
zh: 我们正在取得进展。现在我们知道如何在 Lean 中定义代数结构，也知道如何定义这些结构的实例。但我们还希望把记号同结构关联起来，使得每个实例都能使用这些记号。此外，我们还希望安排成：可以在一个结构上定义操作，并在任何具体实例中使用它；也可以证明关于一个结构的定理，并在任何实例中使用它。

text: In fact, Mathlib is already set up to use generic group notation, definitions, and theorems for Equiv.Perm α .
html: In fact, Mathlib is already set up to use generic group notation, definitions, and theorems for Equiv.Perm α.
zh: 事实上，Mathlib 已经设置好，可以对 Equiv.Perm α 使用通用的群记号、定义和定理。

text: You can check that this is not the case for the additive group structure on Point that we asked you to define above. Our task now is to understand that magic that goes on under the hood in order to make the examples for Equiv.Perm α work the way they do.
html: You can check that this is not the case for the additive group structure on Point that we asked you to define above. Our task now is to understand that magic that goes on under the hood in order to make the examples for Equiv.Perm α work the way they do.
zh: 你可以检查，上面要求你为 Point 定义的加法群结构并非如此。现在我们的任务是理解底层发生了什么“魔法”，使得 Equiv.Perm α 的例子能够这样工作。

text: The issue is that Lean needs to be able to find the relevant notation and the implicit group structure, using the information that is found in the expressions that we type. Similarly, when we write x + y with expressions x and y that have type ℝ , Lean needs to interpret the + symbol as the relevant addition function on the reals. It also has to recognize the type ℝ as an instance of a commutative ring, so that all the definitions and theorems for a commutative ring are available. For another example, continuity is defined in Lean relative to any two topological spaces. When we have f : ℝ → ℂ and we write Continuous f , Lean has to find the relevant topologies on ℝ and ℂ .
html: The issue is that Lean needs to be able to find the relevant notation and the implicit group structure, using the information that is found in the expressions that we type. Similarly, when we write x + y with expressions x and y that have type ℝ, Lean needs to interpret the + symbol as the relevant addition function on the reals. It also has to recognize the type ℝ as an instance of a commutative ring, so that all the definitions and theorems for a commutative ring are available. For another example, continuity is defined in Lean relative to any two topological spaces. When we have f : ℝ → ℂ and we write Continuous f, Lean has to find the relevant topologies on ℝ and ℂ.
zh: 问题在于，Lean 需要能够利用我们输入的表达式中出现的信息，find相关记号和隐式的群结构。类似地，当我们用类型为 ℝ 的表达式 x 和 y 写 x + y 时，Lean 需要把符号 + 解释为实数上的相关加法函数。它还必须识别类型 ℝ 是交换环的一个实例，从而可以使用交换环的所有定义和定理。再举一个例子，Lean 中的连续性是相对于任意两个拓扑空间定义的。当有 f : ℝ → ℂ 且写 Continuous f 时，Lean 必须找到 ℝ 和 ℂ 上的相关拓扑。

text: The magic is achieved with a combination of three things.
html: The magic is achieved with a combination of three things.
zh: 这种机制由三件事结合实现。

text: Whereas an annotation (grp : Group G) tells Lean that it should expect to be given that argument explicitly and the annotation {grp : Group G} tells Lean that it should try to figure it out from contextual cues in the expression, the annotation [grp : Group G] tells Lean that the corresponding argument should be synthesized using type class inference. Since the whole point to the use of such arguments is that we generally do not need to refer to them explicitly, Lean allows us to write [Group G] and leave the name anonymous. You have probably already noticed that Lean chooses names like _inst_1 automatically. When we use the anonymous square-bracket annotation with the variable command, then as long as the variables are still in scope, Lean automatically adds the argument [Group G] to any definition or theorem that mentions G .
html: Whereas an annotation (grp : Group G) tells Lean that it should expect to be given that argument explicitly and the annotation {grp : Group G} tells Lean that it should try to figure it out from contextual cues in the expression, the annotation [grp : Group G] tells Lean that the corresponding argument should be synthesized using type class inference. Since the whole point to the use of such arguments is that we generally do not need to refer to them explicitly, Lean allows us to write [Group G] and leave the name anonymous. You have probably already noticed that Lean chooses names like _inst_1 automatically. When we use the anonymous square-bracket annotation with the variable command, then as long as the variables are still in scope, Lean automatically adds the argument [Group G] to any definition or theorem that mentions G.
zh: 标注 (grp : Group G) 告诉 Lean 应期待显式给出该参数；标注 {grp : Group G} 告诉 Lean 应尝试从表达式中的上下文线索推断它；而标注 [grp : Group G] 则告诉 Lean 应使用类型类推断来合成相应参数。由于使用这类参数的要点在于我们通常不需要显式引用它们，Lean 允许写 [Group G] 并让名称匿名。你大概已经注意到，Lean 会自动选择像 _inst_1 这样的名称。当把匿名方括号标注同 variable 命令一起使用时，只要变量仍在作用域内，Lean 就会自动向任何提到 G 的定义或定理添加参数 [Group G]。

text: How do we register the information that Lean needs to use to carry out the search? Returning to our group example, we need only make two changes. First, instead of using the structure command to define the group structure, we use the keyword class to indicate that it is a candidate for class inference. Second, instead of defining particular instances with def , we use the keyword instance to register the particular instance with Lean. As with the names of class variables, we are allowed to leave the name of an instance definition anonymous, since in general we intend Lean to find it and put it to use without troubling us with the details.
html: How do we register the information that Lean needs to use to carry out the search? Returning to our group example, we need only make two changes. First, instead of using the structure command to define the group structure, we use the keyword class to indicate that it is a candidate for class inference. Second, instead of defining particular instances with def, we use the keyword instance to register the particular instance with Lean. As with the names of class variables, we are allowed to leave the name of an instance definition anonymous, since in general we intend Lean to find it and put it to use without troubling us with the details.
zh: 如何注册 Lean 执行搜索所需的信息？回到群的例子，只需作两处改动。第一，不使用 structure 命令定义群结构，而使用关键字 class 表明它是类型类推断的候选。第二，不用 def 定义具体实例，而用关键字 instance 把该具体实例注册给 Lean。与类变量名一样，实例定义的名称也允许匿名，因为一般而言我们希望 Lean 找到它并使用它，而不必用细节打扰我们。

text: The following illustrates their use.
html: The following illustrates their use.
zh: 下面说明它们的用法。

text: The #check command shows that Group₂.mul has an implicit argument [Group₂ α] that we expect to be found by class inference, where α is the type of the arguments to Group₂.mul . In other words, {α : Type*} is the implicit argument for the type of the group elements and [Group₂ α] is the implicit argument for the group structure on α . Similarly, when we define a generic squaring function my_square for Group₂ , we use an implicit argument {α : Type*} for the type of the elements and an implicit argument [Group₂ α] for the Group₂ structure.
html: The #check command shows that Group₂.mul has an implicit argument [Group₂ α] that we expect to be found by class inference, where α is the type of the arguments to Group₂.mul. In other words, {α : Type*} is the implicit argument for the type of the group elements and [Group₂ α] is the implicit argument for the group structure on α. Similarly, when we define a generic squaring function my_square for Group₂, we use an implicit argument {α : Type*} for the type of the elements and an implicit argument [Group₂ α] for the Group₂ structure.
zh: 命令 #check 显示，Group₂.mul 有一个隐式参数 [Group₂ α]，我们期待它由类型类推断找到，其中 α 是 Group₂.mul 的参数类型。换言之，{α : Type*} 是群元素类型的隐式参数，[Group₂ α] 是 α 上群结构的隐式参数。类似地，当为 Group₂ 定义通用平方函数 my_square 时，我们使用隐式参数 {α : Type*} 表示元素类型，并使用隐式参数 [Group₂ α] 表示 Group₂ 结构。

text: In the first example, when we write Group₂.mul f g , the type of f and g tells Lean that in the argument α to Group₂.mul has to be instantiated to Equiv.Perm β . That means that Lean has to find an element of Group₂ (Equiv.Perm β) . The previous instance declaration tells Lean exactly how to do that. Problem solved!
html: In the first example, when we write Group₂.mul f g, the type of f and g tells Lean that in the argument α to Group₂.mul has to be instantiated to Equiv.Perm β. That means that Lean has to find an element of Group₂ (Equiv.Perm β). The previous instance declaration tells Lean exactly how to do that. Problem solved!
zh: 在第一个例子中，当写 Group₂.mul f g 时，f 和 g 的类型告诉 Lean，Group₂.mul 的参数 α 必须实例化为 Equiv.Perm β。这意味着 Lean 必须找到 Group₂ (Equiv.Perm β) 的一个元素。前面的 instance 声明恰好告诉 Lean 如何做到这一点。问题解决了。

text: This simple mechanism for registering information so that Lean can find it when it needs it is remarkably useful. Here is one way it comes up. In Lean’s foundation, a data type α may be empty. In a number of applications, however, it is useful to know that a type has at least one element. For example, the function List.headI , which returns the first element of a list, can return the default value when the list is empty. To make that work, the Lean library defines a class Inhabited α , which does nothing more than store a default value. We can show that the Point type is an instance:
html: This simple mechanism for registering information so that Lean can find it when it needs it is remarkably useful. Here is one way it comes up. In Lean’s foundation, a data type α may be empty. In a number of applications, however, it is useful to know that a type has at least one element. For example, the function List.headI, which returns the first element of a list, can return the default value when the list is empty. To make that work, the Lean library defines a class Inhabited α, which does nothing more than store a default value. We can show that the Point type is an instance:
zh: 这种注册信息、使 Lean 在需要时能够找到它的简单机制极其有用。下面是一种出现方式。在 Lean 的基础中，数据类型 α 可以为空。然而在许多应用中，知道某个类型至少有一个元素是有用的。例如，函数 List.headI 返回列表的第一个元素；当列表为空时，它可以返回默认值。为了使这件事可行，Lean 库定义了类 Inhabited α，它只是存储一个默认值。我们可以证明类型 Point 是一个实例：

text: The class inference mechanism is also used for generic notation. The expression x + y is an abbreviation for Add.add x y where—you guessed it— Add α is a class that stores a binary function on α . Writing x + y tells Lean to find a registered instance of [Add.add α] and use the corresponding function. Below, we register the addition function for Point .
html: The class inference mechanism is also used for generic notation. The expression x + y is an abbreviation for Add.add x y where—you guessed it—Add α is a class that stores a binary function on α. Writing x + y tells Lean to find a registered instance of [Add.add α] and use the corresponding function. Below, we register the addition function for Point.
zh: 类型类推断机制也用于通用记号。表达式 x + y 是 Add.add x y 的缩写，其中，如你所料，Add α 是一个在 α 上存储二元函数的类。写 x + y 会告诉 Lean 寻找 [Add.add α] 的已注册实例并使用相应函数。下面，我们为 Point 注册加法函数。

text: In this way, we can assign the notation + to binary operations on other types as well.
html: In this way, we can assign the notation + to binary operations on other types as well.
zh: 这样，也可以把记号 + 指派给其他类型上的二元运算。

text: But we can do even better. We have seen that * can be used in any group, + can be used in any additive group, and both can be used in any ring. When we define a new instance of a ring in Lean, we don’t have to define + and * for that instance, because Lean knows that these are defined for every ring. We can use this method to specify notation for our Group₂ class:
html: But we can do even better. We have seen that * can be used in any group, + can be used in any additive group, and both can be used in any ring. When we define a new instance of a ring in Lean, we don’t have to define + and * for that instance, because Lean knows that these are defined for every ring. We can use this method to specify notation for our Group₂ class:
zh: 但还可以做得更好。我们已经看到，* 可用于任意群，+ 可用于任意加法群，而二者都可用于任意环。当在 Lean 中定义一个新的环实例时，不必为该实例定义 + 和 *，因为 Lean 知道它们已为每个环定义。我们可以用这种方法为类 Group₂ 指定记号：

text: What makes this approach work is that Lean carries out a recursive search. According to the instances we have declared, Lean can find an instance of Mul (Equiv.Perm α) by finding an instance of Group₂ (Equiv.Perm α) , and it can find an instance of Group₂ (Equiv.Perm α) because we have provided one. Lean is capable of finding these two facts and chaining them together.
html: What makes this approach work is that Lean carries out a recursive search. According to the instances we have declared, Lean can find an instance of Mul (Equiv.Perm α) by finding an instance of Group₂ (Equiv.Perm α), and it can find an instance of Group₂ (Equiv.Perm α) because we have provided one. Lean is capable of finding these two facts and chaining them together.
zh: 这种方法之所以有效，是因为 Lean 会执行递归搜索。根据我们声明的实例，Lean 可以通过寻找 Group₂ (Equiv.Perm α) 的实例来找到 Mul (Equiv.Perm α) 的实例；而它又能找到 Group₂ (Equiv.Perm α) 的实例，因为我们已经提供了一个。Lean 能够发现这两个事实并把它们串联起来。

text: The example we have just given is dangerous, because Lean’s library also has an instance of Group (Equiv.Perm α) , and multiplication is defined on any group. So it is ambiguous as to which instance is found. In fact, Lean favors more recent declarations unless you explicitly specify a different priority. Also, there is another way to tell Lean that one structure is an instance of another, using the extends keyword. This is how Mathlib specifies that, for example, every commutative ring is a ring. You can find more information in Section 8 and in a section on class inference in Theorem Proving in Lean .
html: The example we have just given is dangerous, because Lean’s library also has an instance of Group (Equiv.Perm α), and multiplication is defined on any group. So it is ambiguous as to which instance is found. In fact, Lean favors more recent declarations unless you explicitly specify a different priority. Also, there is another way to tell Lean that one structure is an instance of another, using the extends keyword. This is how Mathlib specifies that, for example, every commutative ring is a ring. You can find more information in Section 8 and in a section on class inference in Theorem Proving in Lean.
zh: 刚才给出的例子是危险的，因为 Lean 库中也有 Group (Equiv.Perm α) 的实例，而乘法在任意群上都已定义。因此，究竟找到哪个实例是有歧义的。事实上，除非显式指定不同优先级，Lean 会偏向较新的声明。此外，还有另一种方式告诉 Lean 一个结构是另一个结构的实例，即使用关键字 extends。Mathlib 正是以这种方式说明例如每个交换环都是环。更多信息可参见Section 8以及 Theorem Proving in Lean 中关于类型类推断的section on class inference。

text: In general, it is a bad idea to specify a value of * for an instance of an algebraic structure that already has the notation defined. Redefining the notion of Group in Lean is an artificial example. In this case, however, both interpretations of the group notation unfold to Equiv.trans , Equiv.refl , and Equiv.symm , in the same way.
html: In general, it is a bad idea to specify a value of * for an instance of an algebraic structure that already has the notation defined. Redefining the notion of Group in Lean is an artificial example. In this case, however, both interpretations of the group notation unfold to Equiv.trans, Equiv.refl, and Equiv.symm, in the same way.
zh: 一般来说，对于一个已经定义了记号的代数结构实例，再为 * 指定取值并不是好主意。在 Lean 中重新定义 Group 的概念只是一个人为例子。不过，在这种情况下，群记号的两种解释都会以同样方式展开为 Equiv.trans、Equiv.refl 和 Equiv.symm。

text: As a similarly artificial exercise, define a class AddGroup₂ in analogy to Group₂ . Define the usual notation for addition, negation, and zero on any AddGroup₂ using the classes Add , Neg , and Zero . Then show Point is an instance of AddGroup₂ . Try it out and make sure that the additive group notation works for elements of Point .
html: As a similarly artificial exercise, define a class AddGroup₂ in analogy to Group₂. Define the usual notation for addition, negation, and zero on any AddGroup₂ using the classes Add, Neg, and Zero. Then show Point is an instance of AddGroup₂. Try it out and make sure that the additive group notation works for elements of Point.
zh: 作为一个类似的人为练习，请仿照 Group₂ 定义一个类 AddGroup₂。使用类 Add、Neg 和 Zero，为任意 AddGroup₂ 定义通常的加法、取负和零元记号。然后证明 Point 是 AddGroup₂ 的一个实例。请试用一下，并确认加法群记号对 Point 的元素有效。

text: It is not a big problem that we have already declared instances Add , Neg , and Zero for Point above. Once again, the two ways of synthesizing the notation should come up with the same answer.
html: It is not a big problem that we have already declared instances Add, Neg, and Zero for Point above. Once again, the two ways of synthesizing the notation should come up with the same answer.
zh: 上面我们已经为 Point 声明了 Add、Neg 和 Zero 的实例，这并不是什么大问题。再次，两种合成记号的方式应当得到相同答案。

text: Class inference is subtle, and you have to be careful when using it, because it configures automation that invisibly governs the interpretation of the expressions we type. When used wisely, however, class inference is a powerful tool. It is what makes algebraic reasoning possible in Lean.
html: Class inference is subtle, and you have to be careful when using it, because it configures automation that invisibly governs the interpretation of the expressions we type. When used wisely, however, class inference is a powerful tool. It is what makes algebraic reasoning possible in Lean.
zh: 类型类推断很微妙，使用时必须谨慎，因为它配置的自动化会在不可见处支配我们输入表达式的解释。不过，若使用得当，类型类推断是一个强有力的工具。正是它使 Lean 中的代数推理成为可能。

text: We will now illustrate the use of the algebraic hierarchy in Lean by building an important mathematical object, the Gaussian integers , and showing that it is a Euclidean domain. In other words, according to the terminology we have been using, we will define the Gaussian integers and show that they are an instance of the Euclidean domain structure.
html: We will now illustrate the use of the algebraic hierarchy in Lean by building an important mathematical object, the Gaussian integers, and showing that it is a Euclidean domain. In other words, according to the terminology we have been using, we will define the Gaussian integers and show that they are an instance of the Euclidean domain structure.
zh: 现在我们通过构造一个重要数学对象，即Gaussian integers，并证明它是欧几里得整环，来展示 Lean 中代数层级的用法。换言之，按照一直使用的术语，我们将定义高斯整数，并证明它们是欧几里得整环结构的一个实例。

text: In ordinary mathematical terms, the set of Gaussian integers \(\Bbb{Z}[i]\) is the set of complex numbers \(\{ a + b i \mid a, b \in \Bbb{Z}\}\) . But rather than define them as a subset of the complex numbers, our goal here is to define them as a data type in their own right. We do this by representing a Gaussian integer as a pair of integers, which we think of as the real and imaginary parts.
html: In ordinary mathematical terms, the set of Gaussian integers \(\Bbb{Z}[i]\) is the set of complex numbers \(\{ a + b i \mid a, b \in \Bbb{Z}\}\). But rather than define them as a subset of the complex numbers, our goal here is to define them as a data type in their own right. We do this by representing a Gaussian integer as a pair of integers, which we think of as the real and imaginary parts.
zh: 用通常数学语言说，高斯整数集 \(\Bbb{Z}[i]\) 是复数集合 \(\{ a + b i \mid a, b \in \Bbb{Z}\}\)。不过，这里我们的目标不是把它们定义为复数的子集，而是把它们定义为一个自身独立的数据类型。为此，我们把高斯整数表示为一对整数，分别看作real部和imaginary部。

text: We first show that the Gaussian integers have the structure of a ring, with 0 defined to be ⟨0, 0⟩ , 1 defined to be ⟨1, 0⟩ , and addition defined pointwise. To work out the definition of multiplication, remember that we want the element \(i\) , represented by ⟨0, 1⟩ , to be a square root of \(-1\) . Thus we want
html: We first show that the Gaussian integers have the structure of a ring, with 0 defined to be ⟨0, 0⟩, 1 defined to be ⟨1, 0⟩, and addition defined pointwise. To work out the definition of multiplication, remember that we want the element \(i\), represented by ⟨0, 1⟩, to be a square root of \(-1\). Thus we want
zh: 我们首先证明高斯整数具有环结构，其中 0 定义为 ⟨0, 0⟩，1 定义为 ⟨1, 0⟩，加法逐点定义。为了确定乘法定义，记住我们希望元素 \(i\)（表示为 ⟨0, 1⟩）是 \(-1\) 的平方根。因此我们希望

text: This explains the definition of Mul below.
html: This explains the definition of Mul below.
zh: 这就解释了下面 Mul 的定义。

text: As noted in Section 7.1 , it is a good idea to put all the definitions related to a data type in a namespace with the same name. Thus in the Lean files associated with this chapter, these definitions are made in the GaussInt namespace.
html: As noted in Section 7.1, it is a good idea to put all the definitions related to a data type in a namespace with the same name. Thus in the Lean files associated with this chapter, these definitions are made in the GaussInt namespace.
zh: 如Section 7.1所述，把与某个数据类型相关的所有定义放在同名命名空间中是一个好习惯。因此，在与本章相关的 Lean 文件中，这些定义都放在 GaussInt 命名空间中。

text: Notice that here we are defining the interpretations of the notation 0 , 1 , + , - , and * directly, rather than naming them GaussInt.zero and the like and assigning the notation to those. It is often useful to have an explicit name for the definitions, for example, to use with simp and rw .
html: Notice that here we are defining the interpretations of the notation 0, 1, +, -, and * directly, rather than naming them GaussInt.zero and the like and assigning the notation to those. It is often useful to have an explicit name for the definitions, for example, to use with simp and rw.
zh: 注意，这里我们直接定义记号 0、1、+、- 和 * 的解释，而不是把它们命名为 GaussInt.zero 之类再把记号指派给这些定义。为定义提供显式名称常常很有用，例如可与 simp 和 rw 一起使用。

text: It is also useful to name the rules that compute the real and imaginary parts, and to declare them to the simplifier.
html: It is also useful to name the rules that compute the real and imaginary parts, and to declare them to the simplifier.
zh: 为计算实部和虚部的规则命名，并把它们声明给化简器，也很有用。

text: It is now surprisingly easy to show that the Gaussian integers are an instance of a commutative ring. We are putting the structure concept to good use. Each particular Gaussian integer is an instance of the GaussInt structure, whereas the type GaussInt itself, together with the relevant operations, is an instance of the CommRing structure. The CommRing structure, in turn, extends the notational structures Zero , One , Add , Neg , and Mul .
html: It is now surprisingly easy to show that the Gaussian integers are an instance of a commutative ring. We are putting the structure concept to good use. Each particular Gaussian integer is an instance of the GaussInt structure, whereas the type GaussInt itself, together with the relevant operations, is an instance of the CommRing structure. The CommRing structure, in turn, extends the notational structures Zero, One, Add, Neg, and Mul.
zh: 现在，要证明高斯整数是交换环的一个实例出人意料地容易。我们正在有效地使用结构这一概念。每个具体高斯整数都是 GaussInt 结构的一个实例，而类型 GaussInt 本身连同相关运算则是 CommRing 结构的一个实例。反过来，CommRing 结构扩展了记号结构 Zero、One、Add、Neg 和 Mul。

text: If you type instance : CommRing GaussInt := _ , click on the light bulb that appears in VS Code, and then ask Lean to fill in a skeleton for the structure definition, you will see a scary number of entries. Jumping to the definition of the structure, however, shows that many of the fields have default definitions that Lean will fill in for you automatically. The essential ones appear in the definition below. A special case are nsmul and zsmul which should be ignored for now and will be explained in the next chapter. In each case, the relevant identity is proved by unfolding definitions, using the ext tactic to reduce the identities to their real and imaginary components, simplifying, and, if necessary, carrying out the relevant ring calculation in the integers. Note that we could easily avoid repeating all this code, but this is not the topic of the current discussion.
html: If you type instance : CommRing GaussInt := _, click on the light bulb that appears in VS Code, and then ask Lean to fill in a skeleton for the structure definition, you will see a scary number of entries. Jumping to the definition of the structure, however, shows that many of the fields have default definitions that Lean will fill in for you automatically. The essential ones appear in the definition below. A special case are nsmul and zsmul which should be ignored for now and will be explained in the next chapter. In each case, the relevant identity is proved by unfolding definitions, using the ext tactic to reduce the identities to their real and imaginary components, simplifying, and, if necessary, carrying out the relevant ring calculation in the integers. Note that we could easily avoid repeating all this code, but this is not the topic of the current discussion.
zh: 如果输入 instance : CommRing GaussInt := _，点击 VS Code 中出现的灯泡，并要求 Lean 填入结构定义的骨架，会看到数量惊人的条目。然而，跳转到该结构的定义会发现，许多字段都有默认定义，Lean 会自动为你填充。下面定义中出现的是本质字段。特殊情况是 nsmul 和 zsmul，现在应暂时忽略，它们将在下一章解释。在每种情况下，相关恒等式都是通过展开定义、使用 ext 策略把等式化归为实部和虚部的等式、进行化简，并在必要时执行整数中的相关环计算来证明的。注意，我们本可以轻易避免重复所有这些代码，但这不是当前讨论的主题。

text: Lean’s library defines the class of nontrivial types to be types with at least two distinct elements. In the context of a ring, this is equivalent to saying that the zero is not equal to the one. Since some common theorems depend on that fact, we may as well establish it now.
html: Lean’s library defines the class of nontrivial types to be types with at least two distinct elements. In the context of a ring, this is equivalent to saying that the zero is not equal to the one. Since some common theorems depend on that fact, we may as well establish it now.
zh: Lean 库把nontrivial类型类定义为至少含有两个不同元素的类型。在环的语境中，这等价于说零不等于一。既然一些常用定理依赖这一事实，我们不妨现在就建立它。

text: We will now show that the Gaussian integers have an important additional property. A Euclidean domain is a ring \(R\) equipped with a norm function \(N : R \to \mathbb{N}\) with the following two properties:
html: We will now show that the Gaussian integers have an important additional property. A Euclidean domain is a ring \(R\) equipped with a norm function \(N : R \to \mathbb{N}\) with the following two properties:
zh: 现在我们将证明高斯整数还具有一个重要附加性质。Euclidean domain是一个环 \(R\)，并配备一个norm函数 \(N : R \to \mathbb{N}\)，满足下面两个性质：

text: The ring of integers \(\Bbb{Z}\) with \(N(a) = |a|\) is an archetypal example of a Euclidean domain. In that case, we can take \(q\) to be the result of integer division of \(a\) by \(b\) and \(r\) to be the remainder. Note that when a and b are integers, a / b denotes integer division in Lean, and a % b denotes the remainder. These are defined so as to satisfly the following:
html: The ring of integers \(\Bbb{Z}\) with \(N(a) = |a|\) is an archetypal example of a Euclidean domain. In that case, we can take \(q\) to be the result of integer division of \(a\) by \(b\) and \(r\) to be the remainder. Note that when a and b are integers, a / b denotes integer division in Lean, and a % b denotes the remainder. These are defined so as to satisfly the following:
zh: 带有 \(N(a) = |a|\) 的整数环 \(\Bbb{Z}\) 是欧几里得整环的典型例子。在这种情况下，可以取 \(q\) 为 \(a\) 除以 \(b\) 的整数商，取 \(r\) 为余数。注意，当 a 和 b 是整数时，a / b 在 Lean 中表示整数除法，a % b 表示余数。它们被定义为满足如下性质：

text: In an arbitrary ring, an element \(a\) is said to be a unit if it divides \(1\) . A nonzero element \(a\) is said to be irreducible if it cannot be written in the form \(a = bc\) where neither \(b\) nor \(c\) is a unit. In the integers, every irreducible element \(a\) is prime , which is to say, whenever \(a\) divides a product \(bc\) , it divides either \(b\) or \(c\) . But in other rings this property can fail. In the ring \(\Bbb{Z}[\sqrt{-5}]\) , we have
html: In an arbitrary ring, an element \(a\) is said to be a unit if it divides \(1\). A nonzero element \(a\) is said to be irreducible if it cannot be written in the form \(a = bc\) where neither \(b\) nor \(c\) is a unit. In the integers, every irreducible element \(a\) is prime, which is to say, whenever \(a\) divides a product \(bc\), it divides either \(b\) or \(c\). But in other rings this property can fail. In the ring \(\Bbb{Z}[\sqrt{-5}]\), we have
zh: 在任意环中，若元素 \(a\) 整除 \(1\)，则称 \(a\) 为unit。若非零元素 \(a\) 不能写成 \(a = bc\) 的形式，其中 \(b\) 和 \(c\) 都不是单位，则称它为irreducible。在整数中，每个不可约元素 \(a\) 都是prime；也就是说，只要 \(a\) 整除乘积 \(bc\)，它就整除 \(b\) 或整除 \(c\)。但在其他环中，这个性质可能失败。在环 \(\Bbb{Z}[\sqrt{-5}]\) 中，我们有

text: and the elements \(2\) , \(3\) , \(1 + \sqrt{-5}\) , and \(1 - \sqrt{-5}\) are all irreducible, but they are not prime. For example, \(2\) divides the product \((1 + \sqrt{-5})(1 - \sqrt{-5})\) , but it does not divide either factor. In particular, we no longer have unique factorization: the number \(6\) can be factored into irreducible elements in more than one way.

For example, \(2\) divides the product \((1 + \sqrt{-5})(1 - \sqrt{-5})\), but it does not divide either factor. In particular, we no longer have unique factorization: the number \(6\) can be factored into irreducible elements in more than one way.
zh: 并且元素 \(2\)、\(3\)、\(1 + \sqrt{-5}\) 和 \(1 - \sqrt{-5}\) 全都不可约，但它们不是素元。例如，\(2\) 整除乘积 \((1 + \sqrt{-5})(1 - \sqrt{-5})\)，却不整除任一因子。特别地，唯一分解不再成立：数 \(6\) 可以以不止一种方式分解为不可约元素。

text: In contrast, every Euclidean domain is a unique factorization domain, which implies that every irreducible element is prime. The axioms for a Euclidean domain imply that one can write any nonzero element as a finite product of irreducible elements. They also imply that one can use the Euclidean algorithm to find a greatest common divisor of any two nonzero elements a and b , i.e. an element that is divisible by any other common divisor. This, in turn, implies that factorization into irreducible elements is unique up to multiplication by units.
html: In contrast, every Euclidean domain is a unique factorization domain, which implies that every irreducible element is prime. The axioms for a Euclidean domain imply that one can write any nonzero element as a finite product of irreducible elements. They also imply that one can use the Euclidean algorithm to find a greatest common divisor of any two nonzero elements a and b, i.e. an element that is divisible by any other common divisor. This, in turn, implies that factorization into irreducible elements is unique up to multiplication by units.
zh: 相比之下，每个欧几里得整环都是唯一分解整环，这蕴含每个不可约元素都是素元。欧几里得整环的公理蕴含，任意非零元素都可以写成不可约元素的有限乘积。它们还蕴含，可以使用欧几里得算法求任意两个非零元素 a 和 b 的最大公因子，也就是可被任意其他公因子整除的元素。这进一步蕴含，不可约元素分解在乘以单位的意义下唯一。

text: We now show that the Gaussian integers are a Euclidean domain with the norm defined by \(N(a + bi) = (a + bi)(a - bi) = a^2 + b^2\) . text: The argument we just carried out requires viewing the Gaussian integers as a subset of the complex numbers. One option for formalizing it in Lean is therefore to embed the Gaussian integers in the complex numbers, embed the integers in the Gaussian integers, define the rounding function from the real numbers to the integers, and take great care to pass back and forth between these number systems appropriately. In fact, this is exactly the approach that is followed in Mathlib, where the Gaussian integers themselves are constructed as a special case of a ring of quadratic integers . See the file GaussianInt.lean .

text: Here we will instead carry out an argument that stays in the integers. This illustrates a choice one commonly faces when formalizing mathematics. Given an argument that requires concepts or machinery that is not already in the library, one has two choices: either formalize the concepts and machinery needed, or adapt the argument to make use of concepts and machinery you already have. The first choice is generally a good investment of time when the results can be used in other contexts. Pragmatically speaking, however, sometimes seeking a more elementary proof is more efficient.

text: The usual quotient-remainder theorem for the integers says that for every \(a\) and nonzero \(b\) , there are \(q\) and \(r\) such that \(a = b q + r\) and \(0 \le r < |b|\) . Here we will make use of the following variation, which says that there are \(q'\) and \(r'\) such that \(a = b q' + r'\) and \(|r'| \le |b|/2\) . You can check that if the value of \(r\) in the first statement satisfies \(r \le |b|/2\) , we can take \(q' = q\) and \(r' = r\) , and otherwise we can take \(q' = q + 1\) and \(r' = r - b\) (for \(b\) positive; otherwise mutatis mutandis). We are grateful to Heather Macbeth for suggesting the following more elegant approach, which avoids definition by cases. We simply add b / 2 to a before dividing and then subtract it from the remainder.
html: The usual quotient-remainder theorem for the integers says that for every \(a\) and nonzero \(b\), there are \(q\) and \(r\) such that \(a = b q + r\) and \(0 \le r < |b|\). Here we will make use of the following variation, which says that there are \(q'\) and \(r'\) such that \(a = b q' + r'\) and \(|r'| \le |b|/2\). You can check that if the value of \(r\) in the first statement satisfies \(r \le |b|/2\), we can take \(q' = q\) and \(r' = r\), and otherwise we can take \(q' = q + 1\) and \(r' = r - b\) (for \(b\) positive; otherwise mutatis mutandis). We are grateful to Heather Macbeth for suggesting the following more elegant approach, which avoids definition by cases. We simply add b / 2 to a before dividing and then subtract it from the remainder.
zh: 整数通常的带余除法定理说，对每个 \(a\) 和非零 \(b\)，存在 \(q\) 与 \(r\)，使得 \(a = b q + r\) 且 \(0 \le r < |b|\)。这里我们将使用下面的变体：存在 \(q'\) 与 \(r'\)，使得 \(a = b q' + r'\) 且 \(|r'| \le |b|/2\)。你可以检查，如果第一个陈述中的 \(r\) 满足 \(r \le |b|/2\)，则可取 \(q' = q\) 且 \(r' = r\)；否则可取 \(q' = q + 1\) 且 \(r' = r - b\)（当 \(b\) 为正时；其他情况相应调整）。我们感谢 Heather Macbeth 提出下面这个更优雅的方法，它避免了分类定义。只需在除法前把 b / 2 加到 a 上，然后从余数中减去它。
